commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `ORACLE_CHATGPT_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` for exact saved-account selection, refuses ambiguous or missing matches, redacts account identifiers from diagnostics, and adds focused tests and docs.

Reproducibility: yes. at source and fixture level: current main clicks the first detected email-bearing Welcome back element, and the branch harness reproduces ambiguous and substring-collision cases. No high-confidence reproduction has exercised the actual ChatGPT picker after the fix.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer must decide whether to accept the fail-closed compatibility change and require contributor-supplied actual-picker proof; there is no remaining narrow mechanical defect for an automated repair lane.

target.title: [codex] Prefer configured ChatGPT welcome-back account
target.body:
## Summary

- Select ChatGPT's saved "Welcome back" identity only by exact email from `ORACLE_CHATGPT_ACCOUNT_EMAIL`.
- Preserve automatic selection for one unambiguous saved account; fail closed when several accounts exist or the configured account is absent.
- Keep account addresses out of Oracle logs and add focused regression coverage, docs, and changelog credit.

## Product decision

The account preference is intentionally host-side environment configuration, not a CLI flag or persisted session field. This keeps the identity out of command history and Oracle session metadata. Set `ORACLE_CHATGPT_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` on the machine running browser automation, including the `oracle serve` host for delegated browser runs.

## Browser proof

- Existing authenticated Chrome profile: ChatGPT root rendered a composer with no login CTA; the "Welcome back" picker was not currently present, so the real account picker could not be safely forced without changing authentication state.
- Repository-generated picker expression executed in that real Chrome instance against rendered DOM fixtures:
  - substring-collision case selected only the exact second identity;
  - missing configured identity clicked nothing;
  - two unconfigured identities clicked nothing;
  - one unconfigured identity selected the sole account.
- Proof output contained only selection state, counts, and button indexes; account data remained redacted.

## Validation

- `pnpm test`: 1,117 passed, 41 skipped.
- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/browser/pageActions.test.ts`: 54 passed, 1 skipped.
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm docs:check`
- Structured Codex `gpt-5.5` high-effort review: `NO_ACTIONABLE_FINDINGS`.
target.author: derekszen
